name: Events schema (hard-brake, disengage, FCW, etc.)
description: Add a table and sqlc queries for 'events' -- interesting moments detected from parsed logs. Each event belongs to a route and has a type (e.g., 'hard_brake', 'disengage', 'fcw', 'alert'), a timestamp (route-relative and absolute), a severity (info/warn/critical), and an optional JSON payload for detector-specific fields. Downstream features (event-detector-worker, events-ui) build on this.
acceptance_criteria: sql/migrations/007_events.up.sql creates an events table: id SERIAL PK, route_id INT NOT NULL REFERENCES routes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, type TEXT NOT NULL, severity TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info', route_offset_seconds DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ, payload JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(); indexes on (route_id), (type), (occurred_at desc)
A UNIQUE (route_id, type, route_offset_seconds) constraint so reruns of the detector are idempotent
sql/queries/events.sql provides InsertEvent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), ListEventsByRoute, ListEventsByDongleID (paginated, filterable by type and severity via sqlc @arg), CountEventsByType
Migration applies cleanly; go build + go vet pass
